evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my iOS app. Here's my empty view controller: EmptyViewController.swift. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration code into EmptyViewController.swift (DataCaptureContext.initialize + .shared, BarcodeCaptureSettings, BarcodeCapture(context:settings:), BarcodeCaptureListener, Camera.default + BarcodeCapture.recommendedCameraSettings, DataCaptureView, BarcodeCaptureOverlay, viewWillAppear/viewWillDisappear lifecycle), and shows the setup checklist (SPM package, NSCameraUsageDescription, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding ScanditBarcodeCapture and ScanditCaptureCore via Swift Package Manager (datacapture-spm)
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Info.plist
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey: is used (v7 API, not the deprecated constructor)
DataCaptureContext(licenseKey: is NOT present in the new code
DataCaptureContext.shared is read after initialize
BarcodeCapture.recommendedCameraSettings is used (no parentheses — it is a property)
Camera.default is used to obtain the camera
context.setFrameSource( is called with the camera
BarcodeCaptureSettings is constructed and configured
Symbology .ean13UPCA is enabled
Symbology .code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than ean13UPCA and code128 are enabled
BarcodeCapture(context:settings:) is used to create the mode
barcodeCapture.addListener( is called
DataCaptureView(context:frame:) is used to create the preview
BarcodeCaptureOverlay(barcodeCapture:view:) is called
The view controller (or an extension of it) conforms to BarcodeCaptureListener
barcodeCapture(_:didScanIn:frameData:) is implemented and reads session.newlyRecognizedBarcode
DispatchQueue.main.async is used inside the didScanIn callback for UI work
camera?.switch(toDesiredState: .on) is called in viewWillAppear (or equivalent lifecycle hook)
camera?.switch(toDesiredState: .off) is called in viewWillDisappear (or equivalent lifecycle hook)
context.removeCurrentMode() is called in deinit (or equivalent cleanup hook)

evals.prompt: Add barcode scanning to my existing iOS view controller. Here it is: EmptyViewController.swift. I want to scan QR codes and Data Matrix. When a barcode is scanned, print the barcode data to the console.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates BarcodeCapture into EmptyViewController.swift with QR and Data Matrix symbologies enabled. didScanIn prints the barcode data using print(). Shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Symbology .qr is enabled
Symbology .dataMatrix is enabled
No symbologies other than qr and dataMatrix are enabled
BarcodeCapture(context:settings:) is used
barcodeCapture(_:didScanIn:frameData:) is implemented and accesses barcode.data
The scan result is logged (print or os_log)
viewWillAppear turns the camera on
viewWillDisappear turns the camera off
Setup checklist mentions Swift Package Manager / datacapture-spm

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my iOS BarcodeCapture app, I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode in a remote API (an async function call), and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false at the start of the didScanIn callback, launches a Task for the async lookup, and re-enables scanning when the Task completes (e.g. in a defer block).

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set inside didScanIn before the async work
A Task { } or async completion handler is used to perform the async lookup
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = true is set after the lookup completes (defer or final step)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration when a barcode is scanned in my iOS app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeCapture feedback in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns barcodeCapture.feedback.success to a Feedback constructed with vibration: nil and sound: nil.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.feedback.success is reassigned to a Feedback with no sound and no vibration (vibration: nil, sound: nil)
BarcodeCaptureFeedback() empty constructor is NOT used (that is the Android API)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a square viewfinder to my iOS BarcodeCapture preview so users can see where to aim. Update IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularViewfinder with .square style and assigns it to overlay.viewfinder.

assertions.text: A RectangularViewfinder is constructed with style: .square
The viewfinder is assigned to a BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property (overlay.viewfinder = ...)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my BarcodeCapture settings in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter = 0.5 (TimeInterval, seconds) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance before creating the BarcodeCapture mode (or applies new settings via barcodeCapture.apply).

assertions.text: codeDuplicateFilter is set to 0.5 (seconds) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings — NOT 500 and NOT TimeInterval.millis(500)
codeDuplicateFilter is set before BarcodeCapture(context:settings:) is invoked, or via barcodeCapture.apply
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved
